The user wants to use a control for theme color on the index page to select between default and purple theme. Let me first look at the index.html file to see what's there and understand the current theme implementation.

I should:
1. Read index.html to see the current structure
2. Check the CSS files to understand how themes work
3. Implement a theme selector control

Let me start by reading the index.html file.I'll help you add a theme color selector to the index page. Let me first examine the current structure of the `index.html` file and the theme files. Now let me check the index.js file to see how the theme controls are currently implemented: Now I can see the current implementation. The dropdown currently has "Standard (Blue)", "Rose Gold", and "Forest Green" options. I need to add "Purple" as an option and update the JavaScript to handle it. Let me also check how the purple theme CSS is loaded: Good! I can see that the purple theme CSS file exists and uses a class `purple`. Now I'll update the `index.html` and `index.js` files to add purple as an option in the theme selector: Perfect! I've successfully added the purple theme to the theme selector on the index page. The changes include:

1. **Added purple theme CSS** to `index.html` - imported the `variables-purple.css` file
2. **Added "Purple" option** to the dropdown menu between "Standard (Blue)" and "Rose Gold"
3. **Updated the JavaScript** in `index.js` to handle the purple theme class

Now you can select between the default (Standard Blue) and Purple themes using the dropdown control on the index page. The purple theme will be applied when you select it from the "color-palette" dropdown, and your selection will be saved in localStorage so it persists across page reloads.

The user is saying that other pages haven't picked up the theming or the main buttons in the footer. Let me check a few page files to see what's missing compared to keyboard.html (which the user has open and presumably works).

Let me look at keyboard.html first to see what it has, then compare with other pages.I can see the differences. The `keyboard.html` has:
1. The purple theme CSS link
2. `class="win11 light purple"` on the body tag

Let me check a few more pages to understand the full scope: Let me check what the main.css and page.js handle for theme initialization: Perfect! I can see the issue. The `page.js` file initializes the theme from localStorage but doesn't include 'purple' in the theme classes. Also, pages need to:
1. Include the purple CSS file
2. Have the `page.js` apply the purple class

Let me update the `page.js` to support purple, and then we'll need to add the purple CSS link to all pages. Let me start with page.js: Now let me update `page.js` and add the purple CSS to the necessary pages. First, let me check which pages are missing the CSS link and body classes: So only `keyboard.html` has the purple CSS. Let me also check if pages include page.js: Good, pages include page.js. Now let me update `page.js` to support purple theme and add body classes. Let me also check which pages have body classes: Perfect! Now I understand. The pages have `class="win11 light"` but `page.js` should be adding the palette theme class (purple, etc.) dynamically. Let me:

1. Update `page.js` to include purple support
2. Add the purple CSS link to all page HTML files

Let me first update page.js, then add the purple CSS systematically to all pages: Now let me add the purple CSS to all pages. Let me create a comprehensive list and update them all at once. Let me check a sample of different page structures: I see there are different CSS link orders in different files. Let me gather all the pages and add the purple CSS link right after variables.css. Let me use a subagent to systematically add the purple CSS to all pages: Perfect! Now all pages have the purple CSS file included and the `page.js` has been updated to support the purple theme. The theming should now work across all pages. Let me verify with a quick check: Excellent! All pages now have the purple CSS included. The theming should now work properly across all pages. When you select the purple theme on the index page, it will:

1. Save the selection to localStorage
2. Apply to all pages via `page.js` which now adds the `purple` class to both the document root and body elements
3. The purple CSS variables will override the default blue theme

The theme selection will persist across page navigation and browser sessions.

The user wants me to compare the region.html and keyboard.html pages to see what components keyboard has that region is missing. Let me read both files to understand the differences.I can see the key differences between `keyboard.html` (correct) and `region.html` (missing components):

**Keyboard has:**
1. Body tag includes `class="win11 light purple"` - region only has `data-page-id="region" data-oobe`
2. Uses `<mai-button>` web components for buttons instead of regular `<button>` elements
3. In the footer, has a Mode settings button with mode icon

**Region is missing:**
1. The `win11 light` classes on body tag (though the theme JS should add them dynamically)
2. Uses regular HTML buttons instead of `<mai-button>` components
3. Missing the Mode settings button in the footer
